evals.prompt: I just enabled Copilot code review on our TypeScript monorepo. We have packages/api (Express + Prisma), packages/web (Next.js), and packages/shared. The API handles payments so that needs extra scrutiny. We use ESLint and Prettier already. Set up the instruction files.
evals.expected_output: Should generate: (1) Root copilot-instructions.md with universal security, quality, and architecture rules (no imports across package internals). (2) typescript.instructions.md with applyTo **/*.{ts,tsx} for language-level rules. (3) packages-api.instructions.md scoped to packages/api/** with Prisma safety, auth middleware, input validation. (4) packages-web.instructions.md scoped to packages/web/** with Server Component defaults, data fetching patterns, auth checks. (5) packages-shared.instructions.md scoped to packages/shared/** with export surface rules, no side effects. (6) security.instructions.md scoped to auth/payment paths. Should NOT include any formatting/style rules since ESLint+Prettier exist. Every file must be under 4,000 characters. All *.instructions.md must have valid applyTo frontmatter.

evals.prompt: We have a Python Django REST API with PostgreSQL. Devs keep forgetting permission_classes on new views and we get N+1 queries in production constantly. We use Ruff and Black for formatting. Also we have Celery for background tasks and those crash sometimes without proper retry logic. Help me set up Copilot code review instructions.
evals.expected_output: Should generate: (1) copilot-instructions.md with security and performance fundamentals. (2) python.instructions.md with applyTo **/*.py for language rules (type hints, context managers, parameterized queries). (3) django-views.instructions.md scoped to views/viewsets with permission_classes enforcement as top rule, query optimization (select_related/prefetch_related). (4) django-models.instructions.md scoped to models/migrations with migration safety, __str__, indexes. (5) celery-tasks.instructions.md scoped to tasks directory with idempotency, max_retries, acks_late, time_limit rules. (6) testing.instructions.md for test patterns. Should NOT include formatting rules since Ruff+Black handle that. N+1 prevention must be prominent. Code examples must use Django/DRF patterns.

evals.prompt: I'm building a Tauri v2 desktop app with Rust backend and React+TypeScript frontend. The app manages terminal sessions and does file system operations via IPC. We use portable-pty and rusqlite. There's sensitive data (SSH keys). Set up Copilot code review to catch the important stuff.
evals.expected_output: Should generate: (1) copilot-instructions.md with security as highest priority (IPC boundary, no logging sensitive data). (2) tauri-commands.instructions.md scoped to src-tauri/**/*.rs with Result return types (no unwrap/expect), path validation, shell injection prevention. (3) rust.instructions.md scoped to **/*.rs with general Rust safety. (4) tauri-frontend.instructions.md scoped to src/**/*.{ts,tsx} with typed invoke calls, error handling, loading states. (5) react.instructions.md for component patterns. (6) security.instructions.md scoped to auth/security paths. Must include Rust AND TypeScript code examples. Mutex lock scope rules for Rust. No sensitive data logging rule must be prominent.

evals.prompt: We have a production MCP server in TypeScript that exposes SEO tools via the MCP protocol. It uses a middleware pipeline and Zod for validation. Transport is stdio for Claude Desktop. Set up Copilot review to catch protocol compliance issues, response formatting mistakes, and security problems.
evals.expected_output: Should generate: (1) copilot-instructions.md with protocol compliance and security. (2) mcp-tools.instructions.md scoped to tools/**/*.ts with Zod validation matching schemas, isError usage, try-catch requirement. (3) mcp-transport.instructions.md with the critical rule: no console.log on stdio transport (corrupts JSON-RPC), use console.error for diagnostics. (4) typescript.instructions.md for general TS rules. (5) security.instructions.md for path traversal, shell injection, credential exposure prevention. Code examples showing correct MCP response format and the console.log anti-pattern.

evals.prompt: hey we have a next.js 15 admin dashboard with NextAuth RBAC (admin, editor, viewer). devs keep forgetting auth checks on new API routes and server actions don't validate inputs. last week someone accidentally exposed database connection strings in a client component. help set up copilot review
evals.expected_output: Should generate: (1) copilot-instructions.md with security as highest priority — especially no process.env in client components. (2) nextjs-app.instructions.md scoped to app/**/*.{ts,tsx} with Server Component defaults, route protection, metadata. (3) server-actions.instructions.md scoped to actions/**/*.ts with Zod validation, requireAuth() before data access, RBAC checks. (4) api-routes.instructions.md scoped to api/**/*.ts with auth middleware requirement. (5) security.instructions.md for auth/admin paths. (6) testing.instructions.md. The process.env exposure rule and auth-on-every-route rule must be near the top of their respective files.

evals.prompt: We're building a marketing website with Next.js App Router and Sanity CMS. SEO is critical — we need metadata on every page. Devs keep making everything a client component and forgetting metadata exports. Performance matters a lot for Core Web Vitals. Set up Copilot review instructions.
evals.expected_output: Should generate: (1) copilot-instructions.md with SEO as highest priority (metadata required on every page), performance (next/image, Server Components). (2) nextjs-pages.instructions.md scoped to app/**/page.{ts,tsx} with metadata/generateMetadata requirement, ISR with revalidate, Suspense boundaries. (3) nextjs-components.instructions.md scoped to components/**/*.{tsx,jsx} with Server-first philosophy, next/image not raw img, next/link usage. (4) cms-integration.instructions.md scoped to lib/sanity/**/*.ts with defensive CMS data handling, image URL building. (5) a11y.instructions.md for accessibility. Must NOT include formatting rules. The minimize-use-client rule must be prominent.

evals.prompt: I have a Go microservice using chi router and sqlx for PostgreSQL. We also have gRPC services. Main problems: people forget to check errors (just use _ to discard), goroutine leaks in background workers, and no context propagation through the call chain. We have golangci-lint set up. Set up Copilot instructions.
evals.expected_output: Should generate: (1) copilot-instructions.md with error handling as top rule (never discard errors with _), goroutine leak prevention, context propagation. (2) go.instructions.md scoped to **/*.go with language conventions, error wrapping with %w, context as first param. (3) api-handlers.instructions.md scoped to handlers/**/*.go with request validation, response format, context passing. (4) database.instructions.md scoped to db/repository/store dirs with parameterized queries, connection pooling, sql.ErrNoRows handling. (5) grpc.instructions.md for gRPC service patterns. Should NOT include style rules since golangci-lint handles those. Code examples must show Go idioms (not generic patterns).

evals.prompt: We already have some Copilot instruction files but they're not working well. Reviews keep flagging formatting stuff that Prettier already handles and missing the important security issues. We have one big copilot-instructions.md with everything in it — TypeScript rules, React rules, API rules, all mixed together. It's like 8000 characters. Can you fix this?
evals.expected_output: Should: (1) Identify that the existing file exceeds 4,000 chars (content after 4K is silently ignored). (2) Identify that formatting rules duplicate Prettier. (3) Split the monolithic file into focused micro-files: copilot-instructions.md (universal security+quality only), typescript.instructions.md, react.instructions.md, api.instructions.md, etc. (4) Remove all formatting/style rules that Prettier handles. (5) Move security rules to the top of each relevant file. (6) Each generated file must be under 4,000 characters. (7) Should read the existing file first to preserve any valuable custom rules.

evals.prompt: I'm building a Python AI agent that uses mcp-use to connect to multiple MCP servers. We keep having leaked connections when the agent crashes, tool calls hang forever without timeouts, and someone committed an API key last week. Set up Copilot review for this.
evals.expected_output: Should generate: (1) copilot-instructions.md with security (no hardcoded secrets) and reliability (connection cleanup). (2) python.instructions.md scoped to **/*.py with type hints, async context managers, parameterized queries. (3) mcp-agent.instructions.md scoped to agent/**/*.py with async context manager enforcement for MCP connections, MCPToolCallError handling, timeout enforcement on all tool calls, multi-server namespace checks. (4) security.instructions.md with no hardcoded secrets, .env usage, git-secrets or pre-commit hooks recommendation. (5) testing.instructions.md for async test patterns. Connection lifecycle (async with) must be the top rule. Timeout enforcement must be explicit (seconds, not just 'use timeouts').
